Website Redesign

This is the Website Redesign project page. Here you can find details about the project, its features, and how it was developed.

Project Overview

Projekt: Redesign the website of otto.de

Tools: figma (Wireframes, Prototype, User Flows)

Platform: Website

Background

As a second project I wanted to take an existing website and redesign it.

Please note, that I am not linked to otto. I simply chose them, because they are a long existing, strong brand in Germany.

I decided for this company, because when using their website, I felt quite overwhelmed by its structure and I couldn't pinpoint why. This sparked the interest in me to research, if I would notice the same with other shops and if not what they do different and how this shop could improve from their influence.

The result was ….

Because of that, I decided to redesign the website, with the overall goal to bring it closer to the app and to modernize it.

User Flows

Since this is a one-man-project I decided to focus on the following user flows:

show user flows here

Note on Real Redesign

I want to point out though, that in a real redesign, you wouldn't just leave out features, that users might already be used to. This is where user research would come into play. Interviews, surveys and analytics of the behaviour of real shop visitors would be considered to figure out, which features are a must-have, can-do or not-needed type.

Features

When I compared the company to others, I noticed the three following features that I wanted to focus on:

  • Feature: reason:
  • Feature: reason:
  • Feature: reason:

Design

Figma Screenshots and prototype here:

Reflection

Working on this case study made me realize, that creating a style for your shop, which then works for apps and websites, can be very tricky. The reason for that is … Because of my software developer background, I know that changing the entire style of a website is usually connected to a big workload and shouldn't be taken lightly. Therefore I will always advise people to think about a global style, before they start adding an app when a website already exists (or vice versa).

Summary

  • Describe the final product. X is a tool that does Y, for customers. It solves the problem Z by doing A.
